releases.name: Argus v0.3.0
releases.body:
## Argus v0.3.0 — `argus run`: OS-level secret injection

> **Early release notice:** Argus has not undergone a third-party security audit. Use for personal or non-production workflows.

### What's New

#### `argus run` — Transparent OS-level capture (Linux & Windows)
Wrap any command and intercept outbound HTTP/HTTPS at the OS level — no per-library SDK wiring required.

```sh
argus run uvicorn app:main --reload
argus run node server.js
argus run --bucket my-bucket -- cargo run --release
```

| Flag | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `--bucket` | Bucket UUID or name (default: `ARGUS_BUCKET_ID` from `.env`) |
| `--no-proxy` | Inject secrets only, no OS capture (no sudo/UAC) |
| `--dry-run` | Validate plan without executing |

**How it works:**
- **Linux** — eBPF redirector; may prompt `sudo` once per timeout
- **Windows** — WinDivert redirector with UAC prompt for redirector only; CLI stays unprivileged
- **macOS** — `--no-proxy` (secrets-only injection); full capture deferred to M4

Traffic is routed through the existing Argus Proxy MITM stack — placeholders are rewritten to real secrets at the proxy layer.

#### New CLI Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `argus run` | OS-level capture + secret injection |
| `argus status` | IPC reachability check (`--json` supported) |
| `argus sessions` | List active sandbox sessions |

#### IPC v4 — Sandbox Session Protocol
New typed sandbox messages: `sandbox_create`, `sandbox_register_pids`, `sandbox_revoke`, `sandbox_list`. No database migration required — additive sandbox tables only (`003_sandbox.sql`).

#### Windows CLI Discovery (PR #27)
- Registers App Paths for `argus.exe` — no `PATH` modification
- Installs `argus.cmd` shim in `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\WindowsApps`
- Validates bundled sidecars at install time; aborts with a clear error if incomplete

### Platform Support

| Platform | Desktop | `argus run` capture |
|----------|---------|-------------------|
| Linux (kernel ≥ 6.8) | ✅ | ✅ |
| Windows | ✅ | ✅ (UAC for redirector) |
| macOS | ✅ | ❌ Use `--no-proxy` |

### Known Limitations
- Not audited — early-access security-sensitive software
- macOS capture not yet shipped (planned M4)
- HTTP/HTTPS only — not gRPC, DB drivers, or QUIC
- Apps with certificate pinning will fail MITM (by design)
- Audit log coverage remains partial

### Upgrade from v0.2.x
1. Download the platform installer from Assets below
2. Install over v0.2.x (Windows: use `*_x64-setup.exe`)
3. Fully quit and restart Argus desktop
4. Enable Argus Proxy on your target bucket
5. Open a new terminal and verify:
```sh
   argus status
   argus run --dry-run -- echo hello
```

Existing `load_env()` / SDK wiring from v0.2 continues to work unchanged.

releases.name: Argus v0.2.1
releases.body:
## Argus v0.2.1 — Fix flashing console windows on Windows (release builds)

Bug-fix patch. Upgrade from v0.2.0 with no migration or config changes.

---

## Fixed

**Console windows flashing on Windows during IPC and proxy use**

On release builds (`windows_subsystem = "windows"`), each `reg.exe` or `git.exe` subprocess spawned during client identity checks could briefly open and close its own console window — visible as random terminal flicker, especially when using the Node/Python SDKs or proxy-enabled buckets.

> This did not affect `pnpm tauri dev` — debug builds reuse your existing terminal.

### What changed
- **Machine ID:** reads `HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid` via Win32 registry API instead of spawning `reg.exe`
- **Git remote:** still uses `git remote get-url origin`, but with `CREATE_NO_WINDOW` so no visible console is created
- **Caching:** machine ID read once per session; git remote cached per project directory to reduce subprocess calls

### What did not change
- Same fingerprint inputs (MachineGuid, git remote, process path, cwd, cmdline)
- IPC approvals, grants, proxy MITM, and secret injection behavior unchanged
- Linux and macOS unaffected

---

## Upgrade

Download the Windows installer below and install over v0.2.0. Fully quit and restart Argus before testing. No database migration required.

releases.name: Argus v0.2.0
releases.body:
## Argus v0.2.0 — Argus Proxy + Node & Python SDKs

Argus keeps secrets in an encrypted local vault with process-verified IPC and human approval. v0.2 adds an optional per-bucket HTTP proxy so apps run with **placeholders** in the environment instead of real API keys, plus official client libraries.

> **Early release notice:** Argus has not undergone a third-party security audit. Use for personal or non-production workflows. Report vulnerabilities via [GitHub Security Advisories](https://github.com/useargus-dev/argus/security/advisories) — not public issues.

---

## What's New

### Argus Proxy
Optional loopback HTTP(S) MITM proxy per bucket (`127.0.0.1:<port>`, ports 9000–9100):

- Enable in the bucket UI → IPC injects `argus-proxy-*` **placeholders**, never real values
- Per-mapping **allowed domains** (suffix match); hosts outside the allowlist get `403`
- TLS MITM via local Argus CA — trust `~/.argus/ca-bundle.pem` in your HTTP client
- Triple gate on `CONNECT`: Proxy-Authorization token + active client grant + OS-verified PID
- Real secrets are swapped in at rewrite time, just before traffic goes upstream
- Audit events: `PROXY_REQUEST`, `PROXY_HOST_DENIED`, `PROXY_GRANT_DENIED`
- Sign-out stops all proxies; soft lock does not (approvals + proxy stay live while signed in)

| Proxy | Client receives | Typical use |
|-------|----------------|-------------|
| Off | Real secret values in env (v0.1 behavior) | Scripts, local tools |
| On | Placeholders in env; secrets only at rewrite | Apps calling HTTPS APIs |

> Scope: HTTP(S) only — not gRPC or database drivers.

### Official Client SDKs
| Language | Package | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| Node.js | `@useargus/node v0.2` | [node-argus](https://github.com/useargus-dev/node-argus) |
| Python | `useargus v0.2` | [py-argus](https://github.com/useargus-dev/py-argus) |

Both libraries load bucket secrets via local IPC (`loadEnv` / `load_env`) with `.env` fallback and return proxy config. Wiring helpers included for `fetch`, `axios`, `httpx`, `requests`, Anthropic SDK, and LangChain — no global patching.

### Fixes & UX
- **Windows:** No more flashing console windows during IPC/proxy identity checks (Win32 registry API replaces `reg.exe`; git subprocess hidden; machine ID + git remote cached per project)
- Bucket UI: proxy toggle, per-mapping allowed-host editor, inject-proxy-token reveal after save

---

## Upgrade from v0.1.x
- In-app updater works from v0.1.0 / v0.1.1
- No database migration required — proxy fields are additive
- Projects with proxy **off** behave exactly like v0.1

> **Packaging note:** Some asset filenames may show `0.1.1` from CI. Verify the release tag (`v0.2.0`) and checksums below.

---

## Known Limitations
- Not audited — early-access security-sensitive software
- Proxy off → real values in `process.env` / `os.environ` after approval
- Proxy on → placeholders reduce exposure, but plaintext exists after rewrite upstream
- HTTP(S) only for Argus Proxy
- Audit log coverage is partial
- Screen lock → app lock not yet wired

Full detail: [`docs/security.md`](./docs/security.md)

---

## Documentation
| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [README](./README.md) | Quick start and feature overview |
| [Architecture](./docs/architecture.md) | IPC, proxy (§11.5), grants, SDK contract (§16) |
| [Security](./docs/security.md) | Threat model, crypto, limitations |
| [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) | Vulnerability reporting |

---

## What's Next
- Go, Ruby, and Java client libraries
- Expanded audit logging
- Screen lock → app lock integration
- Roadmap toward v1.0 after security review — [`docs/plan.md`](./docs/plan.md)

---

*Built with Tauri 2, React 19, and Rust. Community edition: [AGPL-3.0](./LICENSE). Closed-source/SaaS: [COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md](./COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md).*

releases.name: Argus v0.1.1
releases.body:
## Argus v0.1.1

Bug-fix patch. Upgrade from v0.1.0 with no migration or config changes.

### Fixed
- **Approvals window:** Restores from minimized (or hidden) when a new IPC access request arrives, instead of staying in the taskbar unnoticed.

### Upgrade
Download your platform asset below, or update in-app from v0.1.0.

releases.name: Argus v0.1.0 — First public release
releases.body:
**Argus** is a local-first secrets vault for developers. Store API keys and credentials in an encrypted database on your machine—no cloud sync, no remote API for your secrets. Local apps request access through a process-verified IPC channel; you approve or deny each client from the tray or the main app.

> **Early release notice:** Argus v0.1.0 is the first public build. It has **not** undergone a third-party security audit. Use it for personal or non-production workflows until you are comfortable with the [threat model and known limitations](https://github.com/useargus/argus/blob/main/docs/security.md#18-known-limitations). Report vulnerabilities via [GitHub Security Advisories](https://github.com/useargus/argus/security/advisories)—not public issues.

---

## Highlights

### Encrypted vault
- Full-database encryption with **SQLCipher** (`~/.argus/argus.db`)
- Master password protected with **Argon2id**; per-secret **AES-GCM** at the value layer
- Typed secrets (API keys, passwords, SSH keys, certificates, connection strings, notes, and more)
- Tags, search/filters, expiry metadata, and archival

### Authentication & session security
- Local account registration with **mandatory second factor**: TOTP or platform **biometric** unlock
- **Soft lock** (idle timeout) protects the main UI while keeping approval flows available when signed in
- **Sign out** stops the IPC server, zeroizes keys, and closes the database pool

### App buckets (developer workflow)
- Map environment variable names to vault secrets per application
- Keep only `ARGUS_BUCKET_ID` and `ARGUS_BUCKET_TOKEN` in project repos—not secret values
- Per-bucket client tokens (stored hashed); rotate from bucket settings

### Process-verified IPC & human approval
- Local IPC endpoint while signed in:
  - **Windows:** `\\.\pipe\argus`
  - **macOS / Linux:** `~/.argus/argus.sock`
- Client identity derived **server-side** from OS process inspection and a SHA-256 fingerprint
- Every new client requires explicit approval; returning clients respect per-bucket grant TTL
- Injectable secret types enforced in **Rust** (e.g. credentials and recovery codes are not injected via IPC)
- **Requests window** — compact tray popup for pending access requests (last 15 minutes)
- **Approvals page** — view and revoke active grants (available even when the vault UI is soft-locked)

### Desktop experience
- Cross-platform desktop app: **Windows, macOS (Intel & Apple Silicon), Linux**
- System tray integration: hide on close, quick access to requests
- Settings for security, notifications, auto-lock, and tray behavior
- In-app updates via Tauri updater (signed artifacts)

---

## Install

Download the installer or archive for your platform from the **Assets** section below.

**Requirements:** A local user account on your machine; no cloud account required.

**First run:** Create an account, enroll TOTP or biometric unlock, then add secrets and optional app buckets.

**Developers integrating locally:** See the repository README and `docs/architecture.md` §11. Test IPC with `pnpm ipc:test` from a source checkout. Official **Python/Node client libraries** are planned; this release ships the desktop app and IPC protocol only.

---

## Documentation

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [README](https://github.com/useargus/argus/blob/main/README.md) | Quick start and feature overview |
| [Architecture](https://github.com/useargus/argus/blob/main/docs/architecture.md) | System design, IPC, data layout |
| [Security](https://github.com/useargus/argus/blob/main/docs/security.md) | Threat model, crypto parameters, limitations |
| [SECURITY.md](https://github.com/useargus/argus/blob/main/SECURITY.md) | Vulnerability reporting |

---

## Known limitations (v0.1.0)

- **Not audited** — treat as early-access security-sensitive software
- **Post-injection exposure** — after Argus injects env vars, the client process owns that memory
- **User-approved malware** — approval UI is the last line of defense; always verify the full process path
- **Privileged attackers** — root/kernel attackers can target an unlocked session
- **No packaged client SDKs yet** — integrate via documented IPC or `pnpm ipc:test` from source
- **Audit log** — append-only audit is partial; fuller logging is on the roadmap

---

## License

Community edition source is licensed under **[AGPL-3.0](https://github.com/useargus/argus/blob/main/LICENSE)**. See [COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md](https://github.com/useargus/argus/blob/main/COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md) for closed-source or SaaS use cases.

---

## What’s next

- Official client libraries (Python, Node)
- Expanded audit logging and hardening
- Roadmap toward v1.0 after security review and milestone completion — see `docs/plan.md`

**Full changelog:** This is the initial public release; all features above are new in v0.1.0.

---

*Built with [Tauri 2](https://v2.tauri.app/), React 19, and Rust.*
